entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: The same state/update/view application executes accessible interactions and scoped effects in real browser, desktop, iOS, and Android runtimes.
entries.limitations: No normative cross-target application, presentation, accessibility, widget, or UI test contract exists; current graphics and browser operations are foundation plumbing only.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Deleting every generated platform root and rebuilding from .gc, Genesis manifests, policies, tests, and assets reproduces all target projects without foreign-source edits.
entries.title: Cross-target application and UI architecture

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: The emitted directory serves standards-valid HTML, CSS, assets, routes, metadata, CSP and integrity data and passes real-browser navigation, accessibility, and offline checks.
entries.limitations: The current web target is a CoreForm package and synthetic launcher, not a deployable website, so aggregate deployment reachability grants no web support.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: The deployable site is regenerated from GenesisCode views, routes, styles, tests, manifests, and assets with no authored HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Node configuration, or hosting YAML.
entries.title: Static website output

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A real browser and SSR process execute routing, hydration, forms, workers, storage, WebSocket, offline service-worker, update, rollback, and scoped network effects under declared budgets.
entries.limitations: No web component/style/router/SSR/hydration/PWA compiler exists, and the current edge Wasm export performs no application work.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: All client, server, style, route, worker, service-worker, schema, and deployment behavior is authored in GenesisCode inputs; generated HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and runtime configuration are disposable.
entries.title: Interactive, SSR, and PWA web application

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A deployed process handles real protocol traffic, authentication, persistence, migrations, jobs, observability, backup, restore, graceful drain, update, rollback, and injected faults.
entries.limitations: Individual network and database effects exist, but there is no cohesive typed service, durable data, operations, or authentic service artifact platform.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Routes, middleware, schemas, queries, migrations, jobs, policies, observability, deployment, and tests are GenesisCode-owned inputs with no handwritten Python, shell, SQL migration, or YAML requirement.
entries.title: Self-hosted service and data platform

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A signed app package installs, launches a native window, performs accessible input and one scoped host effect, captures crashes, updates, rolls back, and uninstalls on macOS arm64.
entries.limitations: The desktop target emits a descriptor-style package and launcher rather than a signed native macOS application.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Application logic, lifecycle, UI, permissions, assets, tests, and packaging declarations are GenesisCode inputs; generated Xcode, plist, entitlement, and signing plans are never edited.
entries.title: Installable macOS desktop application

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A native package installs, launches a real accessible window, performs one scoped host effect, records resource and crash evidence, updates, rolls back, and uninstalls on supported Linux hosts.
entries.limitations: No installable Linux desktop runtime, package integration, lifecycle, accessibility, update, or interaction evidence exists.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Application, UI, desktop integration, assets, tests, and packaging are GenesisCode inputs; generated desktop files, unit manifests, and native adapters are disposable and unedited.
entries.title: Installable Linux desktop application

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A signed Windows package installs, launches a real accessible window, performs one scoped host effect, captures crashes, updates, rolls back, and uninstalls on Windows x86-64.
entries.limitations: No installable Windows desktop runtime, package integration, lifecycle, accessibility, update, or interaction evidence exists.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Application, UI, Windows integration, assets, tests, installer, and signing declarations are GenesisCode inputs; generated project and installer sources are disposable and unedited.
entries.title: Installable Windows desktop application

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A valid signed IPA containing executable Genesis application code installs and passes launch, lifecycle, accessibility, permission, storage, network, process-death, upgrade, and rollback workloads on simulator and device.
entries.limitations: The current IPA is a deterministic metadata/source archive without an executable app runtime and therefore provides no iOS product support.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: The Xcode project, Swift/Objective-C adapters, plist, entitlements, resources, and signing plan regenerate from GenesisCode-owned application inputs and are never manually patched.
entries.title: Installable iOS application

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A valid signed AAB/APK containing executable Genesis application code installs and passes launch, lifecycle, accessibility, permission, storage, network, process-death, upgrade, and rollback workloads on emulator and device.
entries.limitations: The current AAB declares android:hasCode=false and contains only metadata and Genesis source, so it provides no Android product support.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: The Gradle project, Kotlin/Java adapters, XML resources, manifest, permissions, and signing plan regenerate from GenesisCode-owned application inputs and are never manually patched.
entries.title: Installable Android application

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A nontrivial game or media tool runs real frame, audio, input, asset, save/replay, and device-loss workloads under declared frame-time, memory, startup, and artifact budgets on every required scope.
entries.limitations: Current graphics support is deterministic headless planning and resource codecs, not a production UI, game, shader, physics, asset, audio, or creative runtime.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Simulation, scenes, UI, shaders, audio, assets, networking, tooling, tests, and packaging are GenesisCode inputs; generated shaders and target adapters are disposable outputs.
entries.title: Cross-target games and creative media runtime

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A reproducible dataset-to-analysis-or-model pipeline executes typed table/tensor/statistics workloads, checkpoints, evaluation, local inference, and resource accounting on CPU and a qualified GPU backend.
entries.limitations: GPU dispatch foundations exist, but typed arrays, tensors, dataframes, statistics, model adapters, datasets, and end-to-end local ML workflows are absent.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Datasets, transforms, analyses, model pipelines, evaluation, services, UI, and tests are GenesisCode inputs with no Python notebook, Python package, or foreign orchestration script required.
entries.title: Data science and local machine-learning platform

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: A reproducible image or package boots on the named board, runs a Genesis service or UI, accesses real GPIO/I2C/SPI/UART under policy, survives power/network loss, and updates and rolls back.
entries.limitations: Linux arm64 is listed as a host tier but no Raspberry Pi image/package, peripheral capability, remote lifecycle, power-loss, or physical-device evidence exists.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Device application, service/UI, peripheral policy, system integration, deployment, tests, and update plan are GenesisCode inputs; generated units, udev, device-tree overlays, and scripts are unedited.
entries.title: Embedded Linux and Raspberry Pi-class application

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: Closed-world compilation emits linked firmware that boots and executes the normative embedded subset on every required architecture with validated semantics and measured flash, RAM, stack, timing, reset, and fault behavior.
entries.limitations: No no-OS language subset, static resource verifier, interrupt contract, MCU code generator, startup/linker pipeline, or semantic validation exists.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Firmware, static resource declarations, interrupts, tasks, peripherals, tests, startup intent, and memory policy are GenesisCode inputs; generated C/Rust, startup, vector, and linker sources are disposable.
entries.title: Constrained MCU language profile and AOT backend

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: Signed BSP/HAL packages compile, flash, and exercise typed GPIO, timers, serial, and at least one bus or radio peripheral on each named physical board with pin-conflict and unavailable-peripheral negative tests.
entries.limitations: No board manifests, BSP/HAL registry, peripheral ownership checker, board conformance kit, or physical board implementation exists.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Board manifests, pin/peripheral ownership, firmware, tests, and resource policy are GenesisCode inputs; vendor bindings and register adapters are audited package internals rather than application source.

scopes.label: Arduino Uno R3 compatible AVR board

entries.title: Representative board and capability-safe HAL families

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: Genesis tooling discovers only authorized devices, flashes and verifies exact firmware, opens bounded serial/debug sessions, captures crashes, and performs interrupted-update recovery and signed rollback on supported boards.
entries.limitations: The repository has no Genesis device command, authorized discovery, flasher, debugger, provisioning, crash capture, OTA, rollback, or brick-recovery implementation.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Flash, debug, provisioning, crash, OTA, rollback, and recovery intents are Genesis manifests and policies; users do not author shell scripts, OpenOCD files, vendor CLI wrappers, or updater code.
entries.title: Device discovery, flashing, debugging, and OTA lifecycle

entries.authentic_artifact_predicate: Ordinary CI runs deterministic peripheral simulation while an isolated rig repeatedly flashes, power-cycles, measures, injects peripheral/network/power faults, and retains signed serial/debug/resource evidence from named boards.
entries.limitations: No board simulator contract, isolated physical rig, power/peripheral fault injection, timing/power measurement, or signed hardware witness exists.
entries.one_language_source_predicate: Device tests, digital twins, fault plans, expected traces, resource budgets, and qualification policy are GenesisCode inputs; lab-control internals remain isolated infrastructure rather than application authority.
entries.title: Physical hardware-in-the-loop qualification
